benchmark_details.name: COM2 (COM plex COM monsense)
benchmark_details.overview: COM2 is a complex commonsense reasoning dataset created by sampling multi-hop logical queries from an existing commonsense knowledge graph (CSKG) and verbalizing them into multiple-choice and text generation questions.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To provide a benchmark for evaluating complex commonsense reasoning abilities of language models.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: Issues with data quality and sparsity affecting query construction.

data.source: Sampled logical queries from the ATOMIC commonsense knowledge graph
data.annotation: Manually validated by crowdworkers on Amazon Mechanical Turk

methodology.calculation: Metrics are computed based on performance on the COM2 evaluation set.
methodology.interpretation: Higher accuracy indicates better reasoning capabilities.
methodology.baseline_results: DeBERTa-v3 fine-tuned on COM2 achieved a significant improvement in reasoning tasks.
methodology.validation: Evaluated through human annotation and comparison with existing models.

targeted_risks.demographic_analysis: Potential demographic biases based on the source dataset (ATOMIC).
